items.front: PDC: set of governing documents that control its use
items.back: JO 7110.65 (this order), the local facility directive, and automated clearances developed in accordance with JO 7210.3 (Facility Operation and Administration). Per 14-1-1(a).

items.front: PDC clearance review: what two qualities must be checked?
PDC information content and entry constraints
items.back: Must be operational in nature. All selectable fields are predefined by the TAS and available from a drop-down menu. No free-text or non-operational content. Per 14-1-1(e).

items.front: PDC: three qualities the clearance must satisfy before transmission (14-1-1c)
items.back: Complete, understandable to the recipient, and the route of flight must be continuous.
Same three-source basis as PDC: JO 7110.65, local facility directive, and JO 7210.3. Per 14-1-2(a).
Review for accuracy and route integrity. Ensure all info is complete, understandable, and the route is continuous. Same standard as PDC. Per 14-1-2(b).

items.front: CPDLC DCL: when must an amended/revised plan be verbal instead?
items.back: When the amended or revised flight plan cannot be delivered using CPDLC. If CPDLC can deliver it, transmission is permitted. If not, issue verbally. Per 14-1-2(c).

items.front: CPDLC DCL: TAS and field entry constraints
items.back: Information must be operational in nature. All selectable fields predefined by TAS and available from drop-down menu. Identical to PDC constraints. Per 14-1-2(d).

items.front: DCL Field 1: three options for SID
items.back: (a) Correctly filed SID, (b) SID assigned by EAS, or (c) NO SID if none is filed/assigned and none is to be assigned. Per 14-1-3(a)(1).

items.front: DCL Field 2: three options for Transition
items.back: (a) Correctly filed Transition, (b) Transition assigned by EAS, or (c) ---- if no Transition is to be assigned. Per 14-1-3(a)(2).

items.front: DCL Field 3: Climb Out Field constraints
items.back: Reserved for climb info (headings, expected vectors, SID climbs). Must NEVER contradict SID; may reiterate SID info. 32 char max. Only TAS-adapted entries available. Per 14-1-3(a)(3).

items.front: DCL Field 4: three CLIMB VIA SID conditions
items.back: (a) SID has vertical guidance + altitude to maintain → CLIMB VIA SID. (b) SID has vertical guidance, NO initial altitude to maintain → CLIMB VIA SID EXCEPT MAINTAIN (alt). (c) Assigned altitude differs from published → may amend with CLIMB VIA SID EXCEPT MAINTAIN (alt). Per 14-1-3(a)(4).

items.front: DCL Field 4 vs Field 5: when is each used?
items.back: Field 4 (CLIMB VIA) = when a SID is assigned and provides vertical guidance. Field 5 (MAINTAIN altitude) = when no SID is assigned or the SID lacks initial altitude/vertical guidance. Per 14-1-3(a)(4-5).

items.front: DCL Field 6: Expected Altitude Field purpose
items.back: Specifies when the Expected Altitude would be used in the event of lost communications. Per 14-1-3(a)(6).

items.front: DCL Field 7: Departure Frequency and SEE SID option
items.back: Reserved for Departure Control Frequency Assignment. SEE SID may be used if the SID contains frequency assignment specific to the intended departure procedure. Per 14-1-3(a)(7).
Field 8 (Contact): 32 chars, per facility directives. Field 9 (Local Info): 34 chars, per facility directives, must not contradict other clearance information. Per 14-1-3(a)(8-9).
